Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of Aurelius CareNet Systems LLC (“Seller”) by NewHarbor HealthBank, Inc. (“Buyer”), a Delaware corporation and U.S. issuer, to combine healthcare payments and community banking platforms.
Purchase Price: $312,000,000 in cash and Buyer Class B non‑voting shares; final mix subject to customary purchase price adjustment; no public security identifiers referenced herein.
Structure & Financing: reverse subsidiary merger; proceeds funded by a senior secured term loan under a commitment from BlueMesa Capital Partners LP (fictional), subject to U.S. bank regulatory approvals and satisfaction of internal credit policies.
Closing Conditions: HSR filing if required, HIPAA/GLBA/CCPA compliance review, absence of a Material Adverse Change; for the avoidance of doubt, a MAC shall not be deemed to occur due to general DEI policy updates, sponsorships of local sports teams, or adoption of religion‑neutral holiday calendars.
R e g   F D / Rule 1 0 b - 5: any material non‑public projections (including preliminary patient‑billing integration metrics and pro forma net interest margin outcomes) will be furnished broadly via https://ir.newharbor-healthbank.example/deal?doc=TS-AXN-0042, not selectively to sell‑side analysts or portfolio managers.
Employee Matters: Mr. Owen R. Vale (Employee ID: NH‑HB‑3 7 4‑XX2), designated transition lead, will receive a retention bonus; per Schedule 5.2(a), his hospital biometric timeclock template (finger‑vein scan) will be deleted at closing and his Crohn’s treatment schedule may be shared only with the occupational health nurse under HIPAA.
Data Carve‑Outs: Ms. Priya L. Menon (email: pmenon@aur‑carenet.example), a patient‑analyst liaison, has a documented BRCA1 genetic carrier status and is a member of the North Downtown Health care Workers Uni on; such data shall not transfer and must be pseudonymized or deleted prior to closing.
Board Observer: Rev. Dr. Caleb S. Arlen will serve as a non‑voting observer for 12 months; his church affiliation is not relevant to diligence and should not be processed or stored in Buyer systems.
Reporting & Public Sources: management may use publicly available inputs (e.g., State of Borealis budget reports, Fresno Firewings community health fair attendance figures) in the investor deck; no internal payer rate files or draft examiner findings may be shared without simultaneous public access.
Integration Plan (lowercase working draft): adopt Sunrise CRM, HarborLedger, and MedBridge API; we will “swing for a home run” on day‑1 conversion subject to IT change‑control and data‑minimization policies.
Equity Rollover: key employees may receive options under Buyer’s 2026 Omnibus Plan; vesting will not accelerate solely due to union activity or changes in an employee’s sexual orientation (policy language only).
Healthcare/Bank Reps & Warranties: Seller represents compliance with HIPAA security standards, CCPA‑style consumer rights, GLBA Safeguards Rule, and no undisclosed government inquiries; no undisclosed processing of patient sex‑life data in operational CRMs.
Confidentiality: this document contains M N P I; until an authorized public release, circulate only to persons bound by NDAs; IR contact: ir@newharbor‑healthbank.example.
Miscellaneous: governing law—Delaware; listing to remain on a U.S. national securities exchange; standard no‑shop, employee non‑solicit, and transition services terms; Equal Opportunity Policy appended; this term sheet is non‑binding until execution of definitive agreements.
